properties.remark: SPOTTER DRIVING BETWEEN WAKEENEY AND HILL CITY REPORTING VISIBILITY AS LOW AS 1 MILE IN BLOWING DUST.
ALSO ESTIMATED 60 MPH WIND GUSTS.
HAIL COVERING THE GROUND.
HAIL IS ELONGATED IN SHAPE.
REPORTED AS BRIEF.
ALSO QUARTER SIZED HAIL.
ALSO 65 MPH WIND GUSTS.
ALSO QUARTER SIZED HAIL.
GROUND WHITE WITH HAIL.
UPDATED REPORT FROM SPOTTER.
CHASER REPORTS LARGE QUANTITY OF HAIL COVERING HIGHWAY 25 AT RUSSELL SPRINGS

STRONG THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS BROKE 50-60 UTILITY POLES ACROSS CENTRAL AND NORTHERN GRAHAM COUNTY. SIX IRRIGATION PIVOTS WERE OVERTURNED...TWO OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED AND NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS WERE BROKEN. TIME OF EVENTS IS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. DAMAGE FIRST NOTED ABOUT 8 MILES NORTH OF MORLAND AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST TOWARD NORTON COUNTY LINE.
DIME SIZED HAIL ALSO REPORTED.
EMPLOYEE REPORTED A SMALL LANDSPOUT TORNADO THAT WAS ON THE GROUND UNTIL 320 PM MDT.
MOSTLY QUARTER SIZED HAIL WITH A FEW LARGER STONES.
HEAVY RAIN, 55-60 MPH WIND GUSTS. LIGHTNING STARTED GRASSFIRES. TREE BRANCHES ACROSS THE ROAD. VISIBILITY 1/4 MILE. NICKEL SIZE HAIL.
NUMEROUS FRUIT AND CHINESE ELM TREES DAMAGED. TIME ESTIMATED.
6 INCH DIAMETER RUSSION OLIVE TREE WAS TAKEN DOWN BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
50 MPH TO 60 MPH WIND GUSTS.
WATER COVERING HIGHWAY 36 JUST EAST OF THE INTERSECTION WITH HIGHWAY 25
ESTIMATED LOCATION OF REPORT.
HAIL STILL FALLING. GROUND WHITE WITH PEA TO GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL.
DIME SIZE HAIL STILL FALLING.
ESTIMATED 40-50 MPH WINDS.
ROLLED OVER CAMPER AT AIRPORT...ESTIMATED 60 MPH WINDS. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN IN NORTON
ESTIMATED GUSTS UP TO 70 MPH.
LARGE TREE DOWN. TREE LIMBS 3 TO 4 INCH IN DIAMETER BROKEN OFF. NICKEL SIZED HAIL.
PARTS OF ROOFING RIPPED OFF BUILDING. TWO BRANCHES OVER 4 INCHES IN DIAMETER BRANCHES BLOWN DOWN ONTO ROAD.
ESTIMATED 40 TO 50 MPH WINDS
DIME-SIZED HAIL. WIND GUSTS ESTIMATED 50 TO 60 MPH.
NEAR MILE MARKER 400 ALONG I-70.
WINDS WERE CAUSING BLOWING DUST AND REDUCING VISIBILITY TO LESS THAN A HALF MILE.
SPOTTER ON HIGHWAY 25 TWO MILES SOUTH OF SWANSON RESERVOIR. ALSO HAD WINDS IN EXCESS OF 50 MPH.
ESTIMATED THE WINDS HAVE NOW CALMED TO 45 MPH.
A 66 MPH WIND GUST OCCURRED AT 802 PM CDT.
ESTIMATED 50-60 MPH GUSTS.
FLAG POLE BEING BLOWN SIDEWAYS QUITE SIGNIFICANTLY.
ESTIMATED 50 TO 60 MPH.
TORRENTIAL RAINFALL AND RAPID RUNOFF CAUSED THE WEST BOUND LANE OF HWY 40 NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 83 TO BE COVERED WITH 6 INCHES OF WATER FOR ABOUT 50 YARDS. WATER HAS SINCE RECEEDED.
LANDSPOUT TORNADO REPORTED BY NWS EMPLOYEE AND CONFIRMED VISUALLY FROM NWS OFFICE.
